data.title: NOW Stock’s Plunge Today Takes 2026 Losses To 45% — Analyst Warns Penalty For Missteps Is Severe As AI Startups Ramp Up Enterprise Offerings
data.description:
Key points:
  • KeyBanc analysts highlighted that ServiceNow missed out on some deals in the Middle East during the quarter.
  • The firm also stated that the company’s margin guidance is declining due to acquisitions, and that ServiceNow’s current Remaining Performance Obligations were skinnier than usual in Q1.
  • Jefferies analysts stated that, following ServiceNow’s Q1 performance, investor sentiment is likely to remain muted until the company’s analyst day on May 4.

Shares of ServiceNow Inc. (NOW) fell more than 17% in Thursday morning’s trade, extending the stock’s 2026 losses to over 45% after the company reported its first-quarter (Q1) results on Wednesday after the closing bell.

The company reported earnings per share (EPS) of $0.97, in line with estimates, while revenue of $3.77 billion beat expectations of $3.75 billion by a whisker.

Wall Street Strikes A Note Of Caution

ServiceNow’s performance during the quarter resulted in a slew of price target cuts. According to TheFly, analysts at Truist warned that the penalty for missteps is severe at a time when frontier AI labs like OpenAI and Anthropic are ramping up their enterprise offerings.

The firm lowered its price target for ServiceNow to $120 from $125 while keeping a ‘Buy’ rating. Truist added that the company's reiteration of its guidance on organic subscription revenue left investors wanting more.

Analysts at Jefferies also lowered their price target for ServiceNow to $135 from $175, while keeping a ‘Buy’ rating. The firm stated in its note that, following ServiceNow’s Q1 performance, investor sentiment is likely to remain muted until the company’s analyst day on May 4.

KeyBanc analysts highlighted that ServiceNow missed out on some deals in the Middle East during the quarter. The firm also stated that the company’s margin guidance is declining due to acquisitions, and that ServiceNow’s current Remaining Performance Obligations (RPO) were skinnier than usual in Q1.

KeyBanc slashed its price target for NOW stock to $85 from $115, while keeping an ‘Underweight’ rating.

Analysts at BMO Capital echoed similar sentiments, stating that ServiceNow’s current RPO guidance was disappointing. The firm trimmed its price target for ServiceNow to $115 from $120, while maintaining its ‘Outperform’ rating.

ServiceNow Flags Iran War Impact On Subscriptions

ServiceNow stated that subscription revenue in Q1 came in at $3.67 billion, representing 22% year-on-year growth. The company added that this would have been higher if not for tensions in the Middle East, while flagging a 75-basis-point headwind to growth from delayed closings of several large on-premises deals in the Middle East.

“There is a slight impact to the guide in going forward in Q2 as a result of the war, because you have to remember, when you're dealing with a sovereign cloud in the Middle East, everything that happens in the Middle East is recognized as on-premise revenue. It's not linear or ratable. It happens all at once,” ServiceNow CEO Bill McDermott said during the earnings call.

How Did Retail Traders React?

Retail sentiment on Stocktwits around ServiceNow trended in the ‘extremely bullish’ territory with message volumes at ‘extremely high’ levels at the time of writing.

One user stated that the NOW stock is still overpriced and added that AI is the future, not software.

https://stocktwits.com/What_Are_Stocks_2/message/651148580

NOW stock is down 45% year-to-date and 47% over the past 12 months. The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Vanguard Total Stock Market Index Fund ETF (VTI) are up 33% over the past 12 months.

The Vanguard Growth Index Fund ETF (VUG) is up 37% during this period, while the iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF (IWF) is up 36%.

data.title: Pace Digitek signs exclusive OEM pact with NEC XON for five African markets
data.description:
Telecom passive infrastructure solutions provider Pace Digitek Ltd on Thursday (April 23) said it has entered into an Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) exclusivity agreement with NEC XON Systems Proprietary Ltd to market, distribute and sell its products across select African markets.

The agreement grants NEC XON exclusive rights for the company’s products, including Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) and related energy solutions, across South Africa, Botswana, Mozambique, Namibia and Mauritius. The agreement was executed on April 22, 2026.

NEC XON is an international entity, and Pace Digitek does not hold any shareholding in the company. The agreement does not involve any related party transaction, and there is no relationship between NEC XON and the promoter or promoter group.

The commercial consideration will be governed as per the terms of the OEM exclusivity agreement. No shares are being issued as part of the transaction.

Last month, Pace Digitek Ltd said that it had received a letter of acceptance worth ₹494.54 crore, excluding taxes, from NTPC Ltd. for a battery energy storage project.

The order covers engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) work for a battery energy storage system (BESS) at NTPC’s Nabinagar Super Thermal Power Station, along with comprehensive maintenance over the system’s design life.

The contract also includes supply, transportation, installation, testing and commissioning of equipment, as well as civil and structural works related to the project.

Pace Digitek said the supply and services portion of the contract will be executed within 15 months, while the maintenance component will run for 11 years.

Shares of Pace Digitek Ltd ended at ₹177.65, up by ₹3.40, or 1.95%, on the BSE.

data.title: Accor first-quarter revenue slightly below expectations
data.description:
Accor , Europe's biggest hotel group by portfolio, reported on Thursday a 2.7% drop in first-quarter revenue, which came in slightly below market expectations.

The operator of brands including Ibis and Novotel has a significant exposure to the Middle East, with the company saying that the region accounted for 8% of its room portfolio as of the end of December 2025, and 12% of room revenue last year.

First-quarter revenue totalled 1.31 billion euros ($1.53 billion). Analysts polled by the company had expected a marginally higher 1.33 billion.

Revenue per available room (RevPAR) - a key metric for hotels - rose 5.1% year on year but fell 9% in the United Arab Emirates.

Accor CFO Martine Gerow said on a conference call that the quarter had started strongly in January and February before a "significant" drop in demand in the Middle East since the start of the Iran war.

In March, the decline was "almost exclusively" due to lower occupancy, while price declines remained limited, she said. Gerow added that the impact of the conflict and its consequences for the global economy "remain uncertain", though Accor still expects growth in 2026.

($1 = 0.8540 euros)

data.title: Gold pares earlier losses on possible Lebanon-Israel ceasefire extension
data.description:
Key points:
  • Lebanon seeks extension of ceasefire with Israel
  • US Treasury yields pare gains after jobless claims data
  • Jobless claims beat forecast, hint at labour market easing

By Ishaan Arora

Gold pared some losses on Thursday on news of a potential Lebanon-Israel ceasefire extension and after Treasury yields retreated from session highs following U.S. weekly jobless claims data.

Spot gold was down 0.3% at $4,722.02 per ounce, as of 11:52 a.m. EDT (1552 GMT), after falling 1% earlier in the day to as low as $4,683.84 per ounce. U.S. gold futures for June delivery fell 0.2% to $4,738.50.

"What provided bid was signs of a potential breakthrough in the Lebanon ceasefire earlier this morning. But that happened to coincide with the economic data release," said Daniel Ghali, commodity strategist at TD Securities.

The U.S. is set to host a second meeting between Lebanese and Israeli envoys on Thursday, with Beirut seeking an extension of the ceasefire, a day after Israel killed at least five people including a journalist in airstrikes.

"Certainly yields and the data are playing a role, but all asset prices, including gold, are moving with headlines," Ghali said.

Benchmark 10-year U.S. Treasury yields let go of most gains, after rising to an over one-week high earlier, decreasing the opportunity cost of holding non-yielding bullion.

More Americans filed claims for unemployment benefits last week than anticipated. Initial claims for unemployment benefits rose by 6,000 to a seasonally adjusted 214,000 for the week ended April 18, up from Reuters' poll of 210,000.

The Iran war has boosted oil prices, with Brent trading above $100 a barrel on Thursday as peace talks between the U.S. and Iran stalled and restrictions on trade through the Strait of Hormuz continued.

Soaring energy prices tend to boost inflation, and as gold is seen as an inflation hedge this also results in higher chances of interest rate hikes dampening the non-yielding asset's appeal. Bullion has fallen more than 11% since the start of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran on February 28.

Spot silver fell 2.3% to $75.91 per ounce, while platinum lost 2.7% to $2,018.10, both having hit more than one-week lows earlier. Palladium was down 4.1% at $1,482.41, after touching a more than two-week low.

data.title: TriLinc Global Impact Fund reports $283M portfolio, $1.21B funded to 103 borrowers
data.description:
TriLinc Global Impact Fund reported $283 million in portfolio assets and $1.21 billion funded to 103 borrowers.

Key Highlights:

  • Portfolio assets of approximately $283 million as of March 31, 2026, including fair-valued investments in borrowers.
  • Weighted average loan size ~ $10 million and weighted average duration 0.4 years, indicating short-term portfolio tenor.
  • TGIF has funded about $1.210 billion in aggregate investments to 103 borrower companies globally, including $104.7 million in temporary investments.
  • Approximately $904.6 million (75% of total invested) has been received in full aggregate transaction repayments to date.

Original SEC Filing: TriLinc Global Impact Fund LLC [ TRLC ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: High-End Cardholders Slow Their Airline Spending — WSJ
data.description:
By Ben Glickman

More American Express cardholders want refunds for their plane tickets.

Chief Financial Officer Christopher Le Caillec says on a call with analysts that airline spending on the company's cards grew in the first quarter, but that consumers and businesses slowed their spend in the latter weeks of March and in April due to the conflict in the Middle East. Le Caillec says the company also saw a spike in customer refund requests.

Multiple airlines in recent days have reported billions in additional fuel costs due to the conflict in the Mideast, leading to higher prices and trimmed schedules. Executives generally expect higher ticket prices to eat into demand.

American Express said that broader spending on its cards rose the most in three years, as profit and revenue topped Wall Street's estimates. Shares are falling more than 3% in midday trading.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Data Center Boom Is a Boon to Texas Instruments — Market Talk
data.description: In recent years, soaring demand for artificial intelligence and cloud computing has caused a massive expansion of data centers across the U.S., boosting the semiconductor business of Texas Instruments. Growth in its data center business has accelerated in the past three quarters, according to UBS analysts. The company's data center revenue surged about 90% year-over-year, analysts say, with the segment expected to continue to grow in the coming quarters. In particular, the company expects its application-specific engagements for AI to ramp up in the second half of 2026, according to Susquehanna analysts.(chris.kuo@wsj.com)

data.title: Corn firms on export demand, wheat up on US weather worries
data.description:
Key points:
  • USDA reports strong corn export sales, wheat gains on US Plains drought concerns
  • Soybeans dip on profit-taking and technical selling after recent highs
  • Higher oil prices and Hormuz blockage support grains, but funds' long positions add risk

By Karl Plume

U.S. corn futures firmed on Thursday on strong weekly export sales and as wheat futures climbed on concerns about drought-reduced production in the U.S. Plains.

Soybeans eased on technical selling and profit-taking after touching multiweek highs in the previous session, as traders assessed spring planting progress and weighed demand prospects amid ample global supplies.

Grains overall remained underpinned by higher oil prices as the Strait of Hormuz remained blocked. But managed funds hold sizeable net-long positions in corn and soybeans, which has left the markets vulnerable to profit-taking corrections.

Traders continued to monitor U.S. weather as some Midwest farmers raced to plant corn and soybeans ahead of rains expected in the coming days, while showers were seen offering relief to only portions of the drought-hit Plains wheat crop.

Chicago Board of Trade July corn (CN26) was up 3/4 cent at $4.63-1/2 a bushel at 11:08 a.m. CDT (1608 GMT), while CBOT July wheat (WN26) gained 8-3/4 cents to $6.15-3/4 a bushel. July soybeans (SN26) fell 5-1/4 cents to $11.74-1/4 a bushel after dropping below technical chart support at its 50-day moving average.

"Corn export sales were at the high end of expectations today, and that's helping support the corn. Also supporting the corn is the rally in wheat, which seems completely weather-related," said Jack Scoville, vice president at Price Futures Group. "But the beans don't have that love."

The U.S. Department of Agriculture said net old-crop U.S. corn export sales last week totalled 1.3 million metric tons, in line with trade estimates, while new-crop sales topped expectations at a marketing-year high of 440,100 tons. Net soybean sales of 364,600 tons were near the low end of trade expectations.
